architecturalImpact.rationale: Additive system following established generator→system→quality→registration pattern. Two new files, three edits. No new shaders, no interface changes, no breaking modifications to existing code.

generator.detail: Return type convention matches PolyhedronEdgeData (positions: Float32Array, randoms: Float32Array, edgeCount: number) from polyhedraEdges.ts. hashRandomFromPosition confirmed exported from subdivideEdges.ts at line 11. Separate vertex result object matches extractVertices.ts pattern.

system.detail: Factory function pattern matches createWireframePolyhedra exactly: config param → returns GeometrySystem & { cleanup(): void; setOpacity(opacity: number): void }. init(scene, seed, params) signature matches GeometrySystem interface in types.ts:32-41.

shaders.detail: All four shader files confirmed present: wireframe.vert.glsl (97 lines), wireframe.frag.glsl (56 lines), wireframeVertex.vert.glsl (107 lines), wireframeVertex.frag.glsl (65 lines). Required attribute aRandom (vec3) is declared. No aEdgeParam/aEdgeTangent needed since electric arc mode is excluded.

qualityProfile.detail: Extension pattern matches existing fields (maxPolyhedra, maxFractalDepth, maxBezierConnections etc). extractSystemConfig switch-case pattern confirmed at quality.ts:145-154.

registration.detail: System instantiation, singleEntries array, and systemRegistry patterns confirmed in main.ts:134-170. Compound mode definition and primaryCountForSystem patterns confirmed in compoundModes.ts:28-68.

riskAssessment.assessment: 7x7x7 grid = 512 vertices + 1176 edges is modest. Existing wirepolyhedra high tier allows 12 shapes × 1920 edges = 23040 edges. Cube lattice max is ~5% of that budget.
Plan uses gridSize^3 as maxPoints proxy but actual vertex count is (gridSize+1)^3. For grid=7: 343 vs 512. Minor undercount but only affects rotation scheduling heuristic, not rendering correctness.

Wireframe shaders use uEnableSlowModulation, uEnablePointerRepulsion, uNoiseOctaves as floats. CubeLatticeConfig includes these fields. No missing uniforms as long as the system binds the same uniform set as wireframePolyhedra (lines 63-124).

suggestions.detail: Consider using (gridSize+1)**3 instead of gridSize**3 for maxPoints in the singleEntries registration to match actual unique vertex count. Difference is small but keeps the budget accounting accurate.

The plan's primaryCountForSystem case returns config.gridSize, but compound mode scaling uses this to estimate point budget. Consider returning (gridSize+1)**3 for consistency with actual vertex count.

new.detail: Self-contained generator with no side effects. Imports only hashRandomFromPosition and createPRNG.
Self-contained system following factory pattern. Imports generator, shaders, Three.js, and types.

modified.detail: Adds 2 fields to QualityProfile interface, 2 values to each of 3 tier objects, 1 case to extractSystemConfig switch. Non-breaking additions only.
Adds 1 import, 1 system instantiation, 1 singleEntries element, 1 systemRegistry entry. Follows existing patterns exactly.
Adds 1 compound mode definition and 1 switch case. Non-breaking additions only.

verdict: Plan is well-structured and follows all established architectural patterns. Shader reuse is the correct call — cube lattice geometry differs only in input topology, not rendering pipeline. Merged vertex optimization (shared vertices at cube boundaries) is clean and avoids the duplicate-vertex problem. No architectural concerns warrant blocking.
